steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Rhetorical Synthesis question. The student wants to describe how scientific understanding "evolved" — that means the answer must present both the earlier explanation and the newer one, showing the shift between them.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Scientists have long sought to determine the origin of the glass, with one study concluding that it formed when ancient grass fires melted the area's sandy soil.: Only presents the 2017 grass fires conclusion. The newer cometary explanation is absent, so no evolution is shown. ✗
  • Choice B — Studies in 2017 and 2021 offered different explanations for the origin of the glass.: Mentions both years but never names either theory. A reader learns nothing about what each study concluded, so the evolution of understanding is not described. ✗
  • Choice C — Mineral signatures of glass samples are consistent with those of comet samples collected by NASA, according to new research.: Only presents the newer cometary finding. The earlier grass fires theory is absent, so no evolution is shown. ✗
  • Choice D — A 2017 study concluded that ancient grass fires had caused the glass's formation, but new research suggests that the glass formed as a result of a cometary explosion close to the desert's surface.: Names the 2017 grass fires theory, then uses "but" to signal the shift to the newer cometary explanation — exactly the two-step evolution the student wants to describe. ✓
That matches Choice D — A 2017 study concluded that ancient grass fires had caused the glass's formation, but new research suggests that the glass formed as a result of a cometary explosion close to the desert's surface..
